item.title: Medicare’s new payment model is built for AI, and most of the tech world has no idea
item.dc:creator: Connie Loizos
item.description: There is no governmental mechanism to pay for an AI agent that monitors a patient between visits, calls to check in, coordinates a housing referral, or makes sure someone picks up their medication. ACCESS creates that mechanism for the first time.

item.title: Musk mulled handing OpenAI to his children, Altman testifies
item.dc:creator: Tim Fernholz
item.description: Altman said that Musk's focus on controlling the initial for-profit gave him pause because OpenAI was dedicated to keeping advanced AI out of the hands of a single person, and Altman, with his experience running the prominent startup accelerator Y Combinator, knew "founders who had control usually did not give it up."

item.title: Anthropic warns investors against secondary platforms offering access to its shares
item.dc:creator: Ram Iyer
item.description: "Any sale or transfer of Anthropic stock, or any interest in Anthropic stock, offered by these firms is void and will not be recognized on our books and records," the company's support page reads.

item.title: Report: Google and SpaceX in talks to put data centers into orbit
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Bellan
item.description: Google and SpaceX are in talks to build data centers in orbit, pitching space as the future home for AI compute, even as costs today remain far higher than on the ground.

item.title: Everything Google announced at its Android Show, from Googlebooks to vibe-coded widgets
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez, Ivan Mehta, Aisha Malik
item.description: Google unveiled its new AI-first Googlebooks laptops, more agentic Gemini features, vibe-coded Android widgets, Gemini in Chrome, refreshed Android Auto, and more ahead of I/O.

item.title: Google adds Gemini-powered dictation to Gboard, which could be bad news for dictation startups
item.dc:creator: Ivan Mehta
item.description: Google's transcription feature will initially launch with Samsung Galaxy and Google Pixel phones.

item.title: The AI legal services industry is heating up — Anthropic is getting in on the action
item.dc:creator: Lucas Ropek
item.description: Anthropic's new tools are designed to help law firms automate specific clerical functions — things like document search and review, case law resources, deposition prep, document drafting, and other related areas.

item.title: Google brings agentic AI and vibe-coded widgets to Android
item.dc:creator: Ivan Mehta
item.description: Gemini Intelligence will also include Gboard-based dictation and form-filling capabilities.

item.title: Google’s ‘Create My Widget’ feature will let you vibe-code your own widgets
item.dc:creator: Aisha Malik
item.description: To create a widget, users will be able to describe what they want using natural language. For example, you could ask the feature to "suggest three high-protein meal prep recipes every week" in order to get a custom dashboard that you can add and resize on your home screen.

item.title: Threads tests a Meta AI integration that works similarly to Grok
item.dc:creator: Aisha Malik
item.description: The feature is designed to help people get real-time context about trends and breaking stories, as well as receive recommendations, all within conversations.

item.title: Dessn raises $6M for its production-focused design tool
item.dc:creator: Ivan Mehta
item.description: A new startup called Dessn has raised $6 million to build AI-powered design tools that work directly with production codebases.

item.title: AI voice startup Vapi hits $500M valuation after winning Amazon Ring over 40 rivals
item.dc:creator: Jagmeet Singh
item.description: Vapi says its enterprise business has grown tenfold since early 2025 as companies shift customer support and sales calls to AI agents.

item.title: Thinking Machines wants to build an AI that actually listens while it talks
item.dc:creator: Connie Loizos
item.description: Right now, every AI model you've ever used works the same way. You talk, it listens. It responds, you listen. Thinking Machines is trying to change that by building a model that processes your input and generates a response at the same time, so it's more like a phone call than a text chain.

item.title: Riding an AI rally, Robinhood preps second retail venture IPO
item.dc:creator: Marina Temkin
item.description: Robinhood files confidentially for its second venture fund, this time targeting growth and early-stage startups.

item.title: GM just laid off hundreds of IT workers to hire those with stronger AI skills
item.dc:creator: Kirsten Korosec
item.description: Some of the positions focus on AI-native development, data engineering and analytics, cloud-based engineering, and agent and model development as well as prompt engineering and new AI workflows.

item.title: Digg tries again, this time as an AI news aggregator
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: In an email to beta testers, the company said the site's goal is to "track the most influential voices in a space" and to surface the news that's actually worth "paying attention to."

item.title: There aren’t enough rockets for space data centers — Cowboy Space raised $275M to build them
item.dc:creator: Tim Fernholz
item.description: The apparently insatiable demand for AI compute has data center entrepreneurs looking to the stars. There's a key problem: There aren't enough rockets to put data centers in orbit around Earth, and they're too expensive.

item.title: Get ready for the whisper-filled office of the future
item.dc:creator: Anthony Ha
item.description: How will work setups change if we spend more and more time talking to our computers?

item.title: Anthropic says ‘evil’ portrayals of AI were responsible for Claude’s blackmail attempts
item.dc:creator: Anthony Ha
item.description: Fictional portrayals of artificial intelligence can have a real effect on AI models, according to Anthropic.

item.title: We’re feeling cynical about xAI’s big deal with Anthropic
item.dc:creator: Anthony Ha
item.description: On the latest episode of the Equity podcast, we discussed what xAI's deal with Anthropic might mean for parent company SpaceX.
